description: Baseline run with real Pi harness runner failed latency gate due missing provider auth causing zero-quality traces and high p95 latency.

asi.hypothesis: Real Pi replay baseline can establish valid policy_score before optimization.
asi.rollback_reason: Run invalid as performance baseline because Pi provider authentication is unavailable; harness calls failed and produced degenerate traces.
asi.next_action_hint: Configure Pi provider API key/login, verify with a single pi_harness_runner case, then rerun baseline.
asi.failure_mode: No API key found for google provider; pi invocations failed across cases.

description: Attempted provider switch to anthropic, but autoresearch support scripts were absent after prior auto-revert, so replay command crashed before evaluation.

asi.hypothesis: Switching Pi replay provider to anthropic (authenticated) will produce valid traces and improve policy_score from degenerate baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Experiment command crashed because required files under mom-autoresearch/ (replay runner/dataset/baseline) were missing.
asi.next_action_hint: Recreate mom-autoresearch scaffold files inside ignored workspace, then rerun anthropic baseline iteration.
asi.error: python3 can't open replay_harness_cases.py (No such file or directory)

description: Switched replay to authenticated Anthropic provider; policy_score improved over degenerate baseline but p95 latency exploded due per-case live model calls, violating latency guardrail.

asi.hypothesis: Using authenticated provider will produce valid MOM-tool traces and raise policy_score versus failed-auth baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Hard latency gate failed; live per-case remote inference made benchmark too slow for adoption loop.
asi.next_action_hint: Introduce fast-mode replay (smaller model, tighter timeout, and/or batched local judge) before next policy mutation iteration.
asi.observation: Quality signal recovered (policy_score 20 -> 37.44) but runtime invalid for guardrails.

description: Fast-fail infrastructure iteration: tightened per-case timeout to 8s to cap replay latency under provider-auth failures; policy score stayed degenerate and p95 still breached gate.

asi.hypothesis: Short per-case timeout will keep benchmark latency within guardrail even when provider requests fail, enabling safe infra baseline runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Latency remained above +15% threshold and no quality recovery signal was observed.
asi.next_action_hint: Establish a local/offline harness simulation mode for baseline and separately gate live-provider runs, or restore provider access before further policy optimization.
asi.observation: Provider failures dominate traces; optimizing policy behavior is blocked by runtime access constraints.

description: Used Pi current default model path (no provider/model switching) to avoid multi-model overhead; partial replay success improved score but high timeout/failure rate caused latency gate failure.

asi.hypothesis: Running replay on current Pi model only will reduce auth/config friction and yield stable traces for baseline behavior optimization.
asi.rollback_reason: 36/50 case failures and p95 latency far above guardrail invalidate iteration quality.
asi.next_action_hint: Reduce evaluation set for smoke phase (e.g., 10 representative cases) with same rubric, then expand after runtime stability improves.
asi.observation: Current model path works for some cases (14/50), but reliability too low for full-suite loop.

description: Smoke-10 stability hypothesis: reduced suite to 10 representative cases to test runtime reliability before full 50-case loop; run produced degenerate all-failure traces with no policy gain.

asi.hypothesis: A smaller representative suite can stabilize replay runtime and preserve valid behavioral signal.
asi.rollback_reason: All 10 cases failed runner execution, producing degenerate traces (no MOM usage signal) and no primary metric improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix runner reliability first (ensure pi session parsing and successful case execution), then rerun smoke-10 before returning to 50-case suite.
asi.observation: CHECK_OK occurred only because failed cases emitted zero-latency fallback rows; this is not a meaningful quality improvement and was discarded.

description: Increased per-case timeout to 30s to improve runner reliability on smoke10; success rate improved materially and policy score rose, but latency and token cost exploded beyond guardrails.

asi.hypothesis: Relaxing timeout from 8s to 30s will recover enough successful traces to produce meaningful behavior signal without violating gates.
asi.rollback_reason: Hard latency gate failed despite better trace completion.
asi.next_action_hint: Decouple quality and latency tracks: keep behavior scoring on fixed offline replay artifacts, run latency gate on separate lightweight probe workload.
asi.observation: Smoke10 reliability improved to 8/10 with strong policy_score jump (20 -> 65.4), indicating scoring pipeline can work when executions complete.

description: Compromise timeout iteration (12s) aimed to balance smoke10 reliability and latency; only 1/10 cases succeeded, yielding slight policy gain but failing latency guardrail.

asi.hypothesis: A mid timeout (12s) can recover enough successful traces to preserve behavior signal while lowering latency vs 30s mode.
asi.rollback_reason: Replay remained mostly failing and p95 latency still far above gate.
asi.next_action_hint: Before further iterations, add preflight health check that aborts run when success rate <70% to avoid noisy degenerate benchmarks.
asi.observation: Timeout tuning alone insufficient; upstream runtime reliability dominates.

description: Added replay reliability gate (success_rate >= 0.70) and reran smoke10 at 30s timeout; reliability improved to 9/10 and policy signal rose strongly, but latency gate still failed by wide margin.

asi.hypothesis: Enforcing a minimum replay success-rate prevents degenerate benchmark passes and yields trustworthy behavior-quality signal.
asi.rollback_reason: Despite strong quality/reliability signal, hard p95 latency gate remained massively violated.
asi.next_action_hint: Split benchmark into two tracks: policy-quality loop (with success-rate gate) and separate latency optimization loop with lightweight prompts/model constraints.
asi.observation: Success-rate gate worked (0.90); this removed false-pass risk from failing traces.

description: Tested concise-response system prompt in Pi runner to reduce latency while preserving MOM behavior quality; replay remained reliable but latency still violated hard gate.

asi.hypothesis: Constrained concise outputs (max 40 words) will reduce latency/token cost without collapsing MOM usage quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Hard latency gate failed despite acceptable replay success-rate.
asi.next_action_hint: Introduce explicit fast model/runtime profile for policy eval or isolate latency metric to micro-probe benchmark not full conversational tasks.
asi.observation: Success-rate remained healthy (0.8) but citation compliance dropped vs prior 30s run.

description: Aggressive low-latency hypothesis (6s per-case timeout + shorter prompts) to meet p95 gate; replay reliability collapsed, triggering success-rate guardrail.

asi.hypothesis: Shorter prompts and 6s timeout can cut latency enough while retaining >=70% replay success.
asi.rollback_reason: Replay success-rate fell to 0.00, making benchmark invalid for behavior optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to stable timeout profile and pursue latency reduction through lighter runtime/model path rather than hard timeout cuts.
asi.observation: New reliability guardrail correctly blocked degenerate zero-latency false signal.

description: Balanced-latency hypothesis: moderate timeout (25s) plus concise speed-biased system prompt to retain replay reliability while cutting p95 vs 30s profile; reliability held but latency gate still failed.

asi.hypothesis: 25s timeout with concise steering can keep success_rate >=0.7 and reduce p95 latency versus previous 30s runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Hard p95 latency gate still exceeded by a large margin.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop timeout micro-tuning; move to dedicated fast-runtime evaluation path (lighter provider/model) or separate quality and latency optimization tracks.
asi.observation: Replay remained valid (0.8 success_rate) with strong policy signal, but runtime cost remained dominant bottleneck.
